commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=20c0db8a8ff13d18026e6a8bd4b106c88badc953)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds Lobster workflow storage and gateway methods, inline/published workflow execution, nested `openclaw.invoke` plumbing through `runtime.tools.invoke`, TaskFlow gateway protocol methods, owner-only tool-policy wiring, tests, and docs.

PR surface: Source +1671, Tests +1138, Docs +127. Total +2936 across 33 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review finding from source inspection: the PR adds `tasks.flows.*` descriptors and handlers, while the PR-head Gateway dispatcher still lists only the three existing task methods. I did not run tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: Needs contributor real behavior proof and maintainer API/security-boundary review; there is also a small concrete dispatcher fix required before the feature can work.

target.title: Add Lobster workflow runtime bridge
target.body:
## Summary

Adds the upstream-aligned Lobster workflow bridge needed by Lobster Builder:

- stores and resolves published Lobster workflow documents through gateway methods
- lets the `lobster` tool run inline YAML or published `workflowId` documents
- adds embedded `openclaw.invoke` and `lobster.workflow`/`lobster.parallel` commands so nested workflow steps run under the invoking agent context
- routes nested native tools through the existing gateway tool-policy path, including owner-only and denylist boundaries
- exposes runtime task-flow status/cancel surfaces used by Builder
- updates Lobster and LLM task docs for the new workflow/native-action behavior

## Why

Lobster Builder now models workflows as OpenClaw-backed Lobster documents rather than builder-only shell pipelines. The runtime path has to preserve the security boundary:

`agent -> lobster tool -> workflow step -> OpenClaw native tool`

Granting `lobster` must not implicitly grant nested tools such as `message`, `llm-task`, `sessions_spawn`, or `nodes`.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Published Lobster workflows can invoke OpenClaw native tools through the embedded bridge while preserving the invoking agent's effective tool policy.

- Real environment tested: Disposable OpenClaw gateways started from the local OpenClaw checkout, using bundled `lobster` plus real gateway plugin loading and real `/tools/invoke` calls. Proof runs used isolated temp config/state and were torn down after each run.

- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran disposable gateway proof scripts from the OpenClaw checkout after applying the Lobster workflow bridge. The live proof path started a gateway, loaded `lobster` and supporting test plugins, invoked `tools.invoke(lobster ...)` through HTTP, and then checked the resulting gateway/bus output before cleanup.

- Evidence after fix: Copied live output from the disposable gateway proof runs:

```text
native-message proof:
  allow agent tools: lobster,message
  request: tools.invoke(lobster workflowYaml=message-send)
  nested path: lobster -> openclaw.invoke -> message(action=send)
  response: HTTP 200
  qa bus: outbound marker recorded exactly once

native-message deny proof:
  deny agent tools: lobster
  request: tools.invoke(lobster workflowYaml=message-send)
  response: HTTP 403
  error: OpenClaw tool "message" unavailable for invoking agent: Tool not available: message
  qa bus: deny marker absent

workflow lifecycle and cron proof:
  lobster.workflow.publish/list/get/delete all returned through gateway methods
  tools.invoke(lobster workflowId=<published>) returned the workflow result
  cron.add/list/pause/resume/remove returned native Cron job state for the published workflow

nested and parallel workflow proof:
  published child workflows
  published parent workflow by workflowId
  parent run returned child output with pinned revision
  parallel parent run returned both branch outputs before cleanup

plugin-hosted builder proof:
  gateway loaded this repo through plugins.load.paths
  GET /plugins/lobster-builder/ returned the hosted Builder shell
  hosted config was tokenless and same-origin
  publish/list/get/run/delete completed through upstream lobster.workflow.* plus tools.invoke(lobster workflowId)
```

- Observed result after fix: The allowed runs completed through the gateway and produced the expected workflow/channel outputs. The restrictive runs failed before the nested native tool executed, with actionable policy errors, proving that granting `lobster` alone did not grant nested OpenClaw tools.

- What was not tested: No additional gaps for this PR proof. Browser UI coverage and CI/typecheck validation are listed separately below.

## Validation

Verified on a throwaway worktree based on current `origin/main`:

- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm exec vitest run src/gateway/tools-invoke-http.test.ts`
  - 3 project shards, 117 tests passed
- `pnpm exec vitest run src/gateway/tools-invoke-http.test.ts src/plugins/runtime/index.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/tasks.test.ts src/agents/tool-allowlist-guard.test.ts extensions/lobster/src/lobster-runner.test.ts extensions/lobster/src/lobster-tool.test.ts extensions/lobster/src/lobster-workflow-gateway.test.ts extensions/lobster/src/lobster-workflow-store.test.ts`
  - 11 files, 207 tests passed
- `pnpm test:extension lobster`
  - 5 files, 54 tests passed
- `pnpm tsgo:core`

Also verified in the existing dirty OpenClaw checkout after carrying back the runtime mock fix:

- `pnpm exec vitest run src/gateway/tools-invoke-http.test.ts`
  - 3 project shards, 117 tests passed
- `git diff --check`

Known local checkout note: `pnpm tsgo:core` in `/home/ubuntu/projects/openclaw` still fails on an unrelated existing `src/gateway/server.impl.ts` channel startup type mismatch. The current-main throwaway branch for this PR passes `pnpm tsgo:core`.
target.author: rendrag-git
